steps.title: Name the missing feature
steps.content: Marlow and Sato place computer games under fiction and cinema, but Tran says those frameworks miss player agency: the rule-and-goal structure that produces enjoyment when players adopt it.

steps.title: Reject softer critiques
steps.content: Choice A calls the frameworks useful starting points, which is not the passage's conclusion. Choice B limits the point to narrative analysis rather than the broader category problem. Choice C claims they overstate influences on narrative elements, which the passage does not argue. The answer is D.
